response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated low-latency distributed systems work at Sociomantic, but the role's core requirement is deep modern C++ or Rust expertise and the candidate's career is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js web development. C++ appears only in a brief National Instruments stint and a bachelor's thesis; there is no Rust experience at all. The candidate has no background in routing or navigation data pipelines, tiling systems, or production-grade geospatial infrastructure, making the gap too wide for a mid-senior hire into a critical data pipeline role.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application development
Low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O, fibers, and high-performance databases
Breadth across systems-adjacent languages (DLang, some C/C++) and Linux environments, showing willingness to work beyond mainstream stacks
concerns: No substantive modern C++ or Rust experience; primary language throughout career is TypeScript/Node.js, which is misaligned with the role's core technical requirement
Zero experience with routing or navigation data pipelines, routing tile generation, or production navigation systems at any scale
GIS work is NGO/hobbyist-oriented (Green Map, conservation data), not production geospatial infrastructure with reliability, freshness, or latency SLAs
